patches.find: Period is trivial (lunar elliptical); dome's local moon cannot produce the observed two-bulge tidal pattern
patches.replace: Period is trivial (amplitude encodes lunar eccentricity e≈0.055); dome's local moon has no eccentricity parameter — N2 amplitude unpredictable from first principles
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon c2: N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 directly encodes orbital eccentricity. Without specifying eccentricity, dome cannot predict N2 amplitude. Period alone is insufficient.

patches.find: Tidal N2 period matches dome model.
patches.replace: The N2 (larger lunar elliptic semi-diurnal) tidal constituent period of 12.66 hours matches dome model, but the dome provides no orbital eccentricity parameter — N2's amplitude ratio (N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 encodes e≈0.055) is completely unpredictable from dome geometry.
patches.rationale: Expands claim to capture eccentricity requirement. N2 is uniquely distinguished by its eccentricity origin; the one-line claim understates this.

patches.find: Period is trivial. Dome's local moon produces a sharp localized spike, not the observed global two-bulge pattern.
patches.replace: Period is trivial. Dome's local moon produces a sharp localized spike, not the observed global two-bulge pattern. N2 specifically requires the moon to have orbital eccentricity — without a stated eccentricity parameter, N2's amplitude ratio (N2/M2 ≈ 0.19) is completely unpredictable from dome geometry.
patches.rationale: Expands verdict text with eccentricity argument per curmudgeon c2 recommendation.

patches.replace: The dome's own OPEN-007 acknowledges that lunar orbital mechanics — including the eccentricity that generates N2 — have no working dome-native explanation, making this a self-undermined claim. See Section 4.5.2 and Doodson (1921).
patches.rationale: Adds OPEN-007 self-admission. Claiming N2 while OPEN-007 concedes lunar mechanics are unexplained is a self-contradiction.

patches.find: Hijacks genuine ΛCDM challenge; dome has no galaxy-scale physics or RAR derivation
patches.replace: Hijacks genuine ΛCDM challenge; dome has no galaxy-scale physics, no RAR derivation, and no ontology for galaxies
patches.rationale: Adds 'no ontology for galaxies'. Dome firmament at 9,086 km means galaxies at tens of Mpc have no location in dome geometry.

patches.find: which predict both poles and the full multipolar structure from a single spherical harmonic expansion of the geodynamo, achieving ~50 nT RMS globally (~0.1% of typical field strength)
patches.replace: which predict both poles and the full multipolar structure from 168 spherical harmonic coefficients derived from geodynamo physics and validated against satellite measurements (Swarm), ground observatories, and repeat surveys, achieving ~50 nT RMS globally (~0.1% of typical field strength)
patches.rationale: Adds IGRF-13 specificity: 168 coefficients, Swarm validation. Makes the contrast with dome's fitted equation more concrete.

patches.find: This WIN is part of group WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063; the same NOAA pole position time series is reused across multiple WINs to inflate the confirmation count.
patches.replace: The dome's own OPEN-007 concedes that polar shift dynamics have no working dome-native explanation — making any sector-specific prediction built on these dynamics doubly self-undermined. This WIN is part of group WIN-040-043, 059-061, 063; the same NOAA pole position time series is reused across multiple WINs to inflate the confirmation count.
patches.rationale: Adds OPEN-007 self-admission. Claims about Siberian sector deceleration while OPEN-007 concedes no polar shift mechanism is doubly self-undermined.
